AND session_id = ?\"\"\",\n                (result_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return {\"result_deleted\": cursor.rowcount}\n\n    # Card cache operations\n    async def write_card_cache(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n        rendered_html: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Write rendered HTML to card cache.\n\n        Stores pre-rendered HTML for a specific result/component/layout combination.\n        Uses INSERT OR REPLACE to update existing cache entries.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result ID\n            component_id: The component ID that rendered this HTML\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket used for rendering\n            rendered_html: The pre-rendered HTML content\n        \"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO card_cache\n                   (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket, rendered_html, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_card_cache(self, result_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all cached HTML entries for a result.\n\n        Returns a list of cached entries, each containing:\n        - result_id\n        - component_id\n        - layout_bucket\n        - rendered_html\n        - created_at\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result ID to fetch cache for\n\n        Returns:\n            List of cache entries (empty list if none found)\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT result_id, component_id, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n                   FROM card_cache\n                   WHERE result_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (result_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_card_cache_entry(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n    ) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get a specific cached HTML entry.\n\n        Returns the cache entry matching all three keys, or None if not found.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result ID\n            component_id: The component ID\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n\n        Returns:\n            Cache entry dict or None\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT result_id, component_id, layout_bucket, rendered_html, created_at\n                   FROM card_cache\n                   WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\"\"\",\n                (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def invalidate_card_cache(self, result_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate all cache entries for a result.\n\n        Deletes all cached HTML for the given result_id.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result ID to invalidate cache for\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of entries deleted\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE result_id = ?\",\n                (result_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def invalidate_card_cache_entry(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate a specific cache entry.\n\n        Deletes the cache entry matching all three keys.\n\n        Args:\n            result_id: The result ID\n            component_id: The component ID\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of entries deleted (0 or 1)\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"DELETE FROM card_cache\n                   WHERE result_id = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\"\"\",\n                (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (unsurfaced results)\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n                    surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE signal_id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Dispatch timing operations (Latency Budget & Instrumentation)\n    async def record_dispatch_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        **fields: int | None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist per-stage timings for one dispatch intent thread.\n\n        Upserts the row so the same intent_id can be written incrementally:\n        process_intent() records the server-side stages it measures, the\n        dispatch caller records sse_emit_ms after the SSE broadcast, and the\n        /api/v1/timings endpoint records client-reported stt_ms/first_render_ms.\n        Only columns passed with a non-NULL value are touched, so a later\n        partial write never clobbers a stage an earlier write already set.\n        ``created_at`` is stamped once on insert and never overwritten.\n        \"\"\"\n        cols = {\n            k: v for k, v in fields.items()\n            if k in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS and v is not None\n        }\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, now),\n            )\n            if cols:\n                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n                await db.execute(\n                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                    params,\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                (intent_id,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n\n        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n\n        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n\n        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n                if since is not None:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params: tuple = (since,)\n                else:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params = ()\n                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n                if not values:\n                    continue\n                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n                result[stage] = {\n                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n                    \"count\": len(values),\n                }\n        return result\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        result_type: str | None = None,\n        card_fallback: bool = False,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n\n        Args:\n            intent_id: Intent thread ID\n            topic_id: Topic ID\n            session_id: Session ID\n            summary: Result summary\n            data: Result data (dict)\n            urgency: Urgency level\n            result_type: Result type for component selection\n            card_fallback: True when no component matched (client renders built-in fallback card)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            result_type=result_type,\n            card_fallback=card_fallback,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n    # Bead watch operations (circuit breaker tracking)\n\n    async def create_bead_watch(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        sla_hours: float | None = None,\n        intent_type: str = \"task-profile\",\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create a bead watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n            sla_hours: SLA in hours (defaults to intent type's SLA)\n            intent_type: Intent type for SLA default lookup\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n        import json\n\n        # Resolve SLA deadline\n        if sla_hours is None:\n            sla_hours = DEFAULT_SLA_HOURS.get(intent_type, 6.0)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        sla_deadline = int(now + (sla_hours * 3600))\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO bead_watch\n                   (bead_ref, refusal_count, comment_high_water, sla_deadline, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, 0, -1, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (bead_ref, sla_deadline, now),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_bead_watch(self, bead_ref: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get bead watch row by bead_ref.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM bead_watch WHERE bead_ref = ?\",\n                (bead_ref,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_bead_watch_refusal(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        refusal_reason: str,\n        comment_index: int,\n        refusal_count_add: int = 1,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update bead watch with a new refusal.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID\n            refusal_reason: The refusal reason from REFUSED: comment\n            comment_index: The comment high-water mark to advance to\n            refusal_count_add: Number of refusals to add (default 1, but can be higher\n                              if multiple refusals are found in one tick)\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Increment refusal_count by refusal_count_add, update last_refusal_*, advance high-water\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"UPDATE bead_watch\n                   SET refusal_count = refusal_count + ?,\n                       last_refusal_reason = ?,\n                       last_refusal_at = ?,\n                       comment_high_water = ?\n                   WHERE bead_ref = ?\"\"\",\n                (refusal_count_add, refusal_reason, now, comment_index, bead_ref),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def update_bead_watch_comment_high_water(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        comment_index: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update only the comment high-water mark (for non-refusal comments).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE bead_watch SET comment_high_water = ? WHERE bead_ref = ?\",\n                (comment_index, bead_ref),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def fence_bead(self, bead_ref: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a bead as fenced (circuit breaker tripped).\n\n        Sets fenced_at timestamp. Caller is responsible for:\n        - Running `bf update --status blocked` on the bead\n        - Setting intent status to 'stuck'\n        - Creating a stuck card\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE bead_watch SET fenced_at = ? WHERE bead_ref = ?\",\n                (now, bead_ref),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def flag_sla(self, bead_ref: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark SLA as flagged (bead past its deadline).\"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE bead_watch SET sla_flagged_at = ? WHERE bead_ref = ?\",\n                (now, bead_ref),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_open_watched_beads(self) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all watched beads that are not yet fenced.\n\n        Returns list of bead_ref, sla_deadline, refusal_count, etc.\n        for all beads where fenced_at IS NULL.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n                   WHERE fenced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_beads_past_sla(self) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all watched beads that have passed their SLA deadline but not yet flagged.\n\n        Returns beads where sla_deadline < now AND sla_flagged_at IS NULL.\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n                   WHERE sla_deadline < ? AND sla_flagged_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY sla_deadline ASC\"\"\",\n                (now,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_beads_needing_fencing(self) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get beads that meet fencing criteria but are not yet fenced.\n\n        Returns beads where either:\n        - refusal_count >= 3 (CIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD)\n        - OR age > 24h without progress (CIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS)\n\n        Age is computed as: (now - last_refusal_at) > threshold when there are refusals,\n        or (now - created_at) > threshold when there are no refusals yet.\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        age_threshold_seconds = int(CIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS * 3600)\n        refusal_threshold = CIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Beads needing fencing based on refusal count OR age\n            # Age logic: if last_refusal_at is set, check time since last refusal;\n            # otherwise check time since creation.\n            async with db.execute(\n                f\"\"\"SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n                   WHERE fenced_at IS NULL\n                     AND (\n                       refusal_count >= {refusal_threshold}\n                       OR (\n                         CASE\n                           WHEN last_refusal_at IS NOT NULL\n                           THEN ({now} - last_refusal_at) > {age_threshold_seconds}\n                           ELSE ({now} - created_at) > {age_threshold_seconds}\n                         END\n                       )\n                     )\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def delete_bead_watch(self, bead_ref: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Delete a bead watch row (when bead is closed/resolved).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM bead_watch WHERE bead_ref = ?\",\n                (bead_ref,),\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_fenced_beads_for_session(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all fenced beads for a session.\n\n        Returns bead_watch rows where fenced_at IS NOT NULL and the bead\n        is associated with an intent from the given session.\n\n        Args:\n            session_id: The session ID to filter by\n\n        Returns:\n            List of fenced bead_watch rows with intent context\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT bw.*, i.id as intent_id, i.topic_id, i.project_slug\n                   FROM bead_watch bw\n                   INNER JOIN intents i ON i.bead_ref = bw.bead_ref\n                   WHERE i.session_id = ? AND bw.fenced_at IS NOT NULL\n                   ORDER BY bw.fenced_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\n\n    When db_path is not given, resolves in this order:\n      1. ADC_DB_PATH env var (used by tests to isolate from production data)\n      2. DEFAULT_DB_PATH (data/session.db)\n    \"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            env_path = os.environ.get(\"ADC_DB_PATH\")\n            db_path = Path(env_path) if env_path else DEFAULT_DB_PATH\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":1904,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1904}}}

data.\n\n    This migration is idempotent - it uses INSERT OR IGNORE to skip\n    existing patterns, so it can be run multiple times safely.\n\n    Args:\n        db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n    \"\"\"\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)\n    try:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n        # Check if component_usage_patterns table exists\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            SELECT name FROM sqlite_master\n            WHERE type='table' AND name='component_usage_patterns'\n        \"\"\")\n\n        if not cursor.fetchone():\n            logger.warning(\"Migration 003: component_usage_patterns table does not exist, run migration 002 first\")\n            return\n\n        # Check if seed data already exists\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM component_usage_patterns\")\n        existing_count = cursor.fetchone()[0]\n\n        if existing_count >= len(SEED_PATTERNS):\n            logger.info(f\"Migration 003: Seed data already exists ({existing_count} patterns), skipping\")\n            return\n\n        logger.info(f\"Migration 003: Seeding component_usage_patterns with {len(SEED_PATTERNS)} patterns\")\n\n        # Insert seed patterns using INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotence\n        insert_count = 0\n        for result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count in SEED_PATTERNS:\n            try:\n                cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n                    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO component_usage_patterns\n                    (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count, updated_at)\n                    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)\n                \"\"\", (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, match_score, sample_count))\n                if cursor.rowcount > 0:\n                    insert_count += 1\n            except sqlite3.Error as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to insert pattern ({result_type}, {component_id}, {layout_bucket}): {e}\")\n\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Migration 003: Successfully seeded {insert_count} patterns\")\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        conn.rollback()\n        logger.error(f\"Migration 003 failed: {e}\")\n        raise\n    finally:\n        conn.close()\n\n\ndef migrate_down(db_path: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Remove all seed pattern data from component_usage_patterns table.\n\n    WARNING: This will remove all pattern data that matches the seed set.\n    Use with caution.\n\n    Args:\n        db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n    \"\"\"\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)\n    try:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n        # Check if component_usage_patterns table exists\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            SELECT name FROM sqlite_master\n            WHERE type='table' AND name='component_usage_patterns'\n        \"\"\")\n\n        if not cursor.fetchone():\n            logger.info(\"Migration 003 down: component_usage_patterns table does not exist, skipping\")\n            return\n\n        logger.warning(\"Migration 003 down: Removing all seed pattern data\")\n\n        # Delete all patterns that match our seed set\n        # We delete by (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket) tuples\n        delete_count = 0\n        for result_type, component_id, layout_bucket, _, _ in SEED_PATTERNS:\n            cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n                DELETE FROM component_usage_patterns\n                WHERE result_type = ? AND component_id = ? AND layout_bucket = ?\n            \"\"\", (result_type, component_id, layout_bucket))\n            delete_count += cursor.rowcount\n\n        conn.commit()\n        logger.info(f\"Migration 003 down: Successfully removed {delete_count} patterns\")\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        conn.rollback()\n        logger.error(f\"Migration 003 down failed: {e}\")\n        raise\n    finally:\n        conn.close()\n\n\ndef get_migration_version(db_path: Path) -> Optional[int]:\n    \"\"\"Get the current migration version from the database.\n\n    Returns None if no migrations have been run.\n\n    Args:\n        db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n\n    Returns:\n        The current migration version, or None if no migrations table exists\n    \"\"\"\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)\n    try:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n        # Check if migrations table exists\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            SELECT name FROM sqlite_master\n            WHERE type='table' AND name='schema_migrations'\n        \"\"\")\n        if not cursor.fetchone():\n            return None\n\n        # Get current version\n        cursor.execute(\"SELECT version FROM schema_migrations WHERE id = 1\")\n        result = cursor.fetchone()\n        return result[0] if result else None\n\n    finally:\n        conn.close()\n\n\ndef set_migration_version(db_path: Path, version: int) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Set the current migration version in the database.\n\n    Args:\n        db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n        version: The migration version to set\n    \"\"\"\n    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)\n    try:\n        cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n        # Create migrations table if it doesn't exist\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (\n                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1),\n                version INTEGER NOT NULL,\n                applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n            )\n        \"\"\")\n\n        # Update or insert version\n        now = int(__import__('time').time())\n        cursor.execute(\"\"\"\n            INSERT OR REPLACE INTO schema_migrations (id, version, applied_at)\n            VALUES (1, ?, ?)\n        \"\"\", (version, now))\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n    finally:\n        conn.close()\n\n\ndef run_migration(db_path: Path, target_version: Optional[int] = None) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Run migrations to bring the database to the target version.\n\n    Args:\n        db_path: Path to the SQLite database file\n        target_version: Target migration version (None = migrate to latest)\n    \"\"\"\n    current_version = get_migration_version(db_path) or 0\n    latest_version = 3  # This migration file\n\n    if target_version is None:\n        target_version = latest_version\n\n    if current_version == target_version:\n        logger.info(f\"Database is already at version {target_version}\")\n        return\n\n    if current_version < target_version:\n        # Migrate up\n        if target_version >= 3 and current_version < 3:\n            migrate_up(db_path)\n            set_migration_version(db_path, 3)\n    else:\n        # Migrate down\n        if target_version < 3 and current_version >= 3:\n            migrate_down(db_path)\n            set_migration_version(db_path, 2)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Allow running the migration directly from command line\n    import sys\n\n    db_path_arg = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\"\n    db_path = Path(db_path_arg)\n\n    action = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else \"up\"\n\n    if action == \"up\":\n        migrate_up(db_path)\n        version = get_migration_version(db_path) or 0\n        if version < 3:\n            set_migration_version(db_path, 3)\n    elif action == \"down\":\n        migrate_down(db_path)\n        set_migration_version(db_path, 2)\n    else:\n        print(f\"Unknown action: {action}\")\n        print(\"Usage: python seed_component_patterns.py <db_path> [up|down]\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n","numLines":330,"startLine":1,"totalLines":330}}}

Only return if we have reasonable confidence\n        if best_score >= 0.5:\n            return best_component\n\n        return None\n\n    def select_component_for_result_type(\n        self,\n        result_type: str,\n        match_threshold: float = 0.7,\n        layout_bucket: str = 'normal',\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"Deterministic hot-path selection — the dispatch card selector.\n\n        Returns the highest-``match_score`` component mapped to ``result_type``\n        in ``component_usage_patterns`` whose score clears ``match_threshold``,\n        or None. No LLM, no semantic fallback, no generation (plan: The Hot Path\n        / UI-Regen Agent — \"highest match_score in component_usage_patterns for\n        the result_type, no LLM call\"). A None return is the signal that the\n        result must fall to the built-in generic fallback card.\n\n        Distinct from :meth:`find_best_component`, which the *async* UI-regen\n        agent uses to steward the library (it may fall back to semantic search\n        and ultimately generate a new component). The hot path must never do\n        either — it only reads the recorded mappings.\n\n        Args:\n            result_type: The result type to match against\n            match_threshold: Minimum score threshold (default 0.7)\n            layout_bucket: The layout bucket (default 'normal')\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n        pattern_row = conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT component_id, match_score\n            FROM component_usage_patterns\n            WHERE result_type = ? AND layout_bucket = ? AND match_score >= ?\n            ORDER BY match_score DESC, sample_count DESC\n            LIMIT 1\n            \"\"\",\n            (result_type, layout_bucket, match_threshold),\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not pattern_row:\n            return None\n\n        return self.get_component(pattern_row[0])\n\n    def _semantic_score(self, result_type: str, comp_name: str, comp_description: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"\n        Calculate semantic match score.\n\n        Simple keyword-based scoring. In production, use embeddings.\n        \"\"\"\n        result_type_lower = result_type.lower()\n        comp_name_lower = comp_name.lower()\n        comp_desc_lower = comp_description.lower()\n\n        score = 0.0\n\n        # Exact name match\n        if result_type_lower == comp_name_lower:\n            return 1.0\n\n        # Name contains result type\n        if result_type_lower in comp_name_lower:\n            score += 0.7\n\n        # Description contains keywords from result type\n        result_keywords = result_type_lower.replace('-', ' ').split()\n        for keyword in result_keywords:\n            if keyword in comp_desc_lower:\n                score += 0.1\n\n        return min(score, 1.0)\n\n    def update_component(\n        self,\n        component_id: str,\n        html_template: str,\n        change_note: str\n    ) -> Optional[Component]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update a component to a new version.\n\n        Invalidates all cached cards using this component.\n\n        Args:\n            component_id: The component to update\n            html_template: The new template\n            change_note: Why this change was made\n\n        Returns:\n            The updated Component, or None if not found\n        \"\"\"\n        conn = self._get_conn()\n\n        # Get current version\n        row = conn.execute(\n            \"SELECT version FROM components WHERE id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        ).fetchone()\n\n        if not row:\n            return None\n\n        new_version = row[0] + 1\n        now = int(time.time())\n\n        # Update component\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            UPDATE components\n            SET html_template = ?, version = ?, last_used = ?\n            WHERE id = ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (html_template, new_version, now, component_id)\n        )\n\n        # Add version history\n        conn.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO component_versions (component_id, version, html_template, created_at, change_note)\n            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\n            \"\"\",\n            (component_id, new_version, html_template, now, change_note)\n        )\n\n        # Invalidate cached cards\n        conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM card_cache WHERE component_id = ?\",\n            (component_id,)\n        )\n\n        conn.commit()\n\n        return self.get_component(component_id)\n\n    def cache_card(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        component_id: str,\n        component_version: int,\n        layout_bucket: str,\n        rendered_html: str","numLines":150,"startLine":300,"totalLines":658}}}

\"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":349,"startLine":1,"totalLines":349}}}

Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # Record usage pattern (hot-path match is high confidence, so score=1.0)\n    lib.record_usage_pattern(\n        component_id=component.id,\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_score=1.0,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"select_rendered_card: rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n        result_id,\n        component.id,\n        component.version,\n        result_type,\n    )\n\n    return rendered_html\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"066cb06f-82b4-4712-aa0a-3cea8ab7b187","uuid":"eca83077-7a6b-447d-947b-5e23e1a1a1b0","timestamp":"2026-07-23T17:18:16.596Z"}

Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    #
